title: Duong Dinh Nghe and the 3,000 Adopted Sons
summary: The story of persistent force preparation and loyalty to protect the nation's autonomy.

slides.text: Do you know how to build a powerful army? Duong Dinh Nghe had a very special way: he adopted 3,000 talented people as his sons!
slides.imagePrompt: Duong Dinh Nghe gathering many young warriors around him, fatherly and strong look, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: These 3,000 adopted sons practiced martial arts together, loved each other like biological brothers, and were wholeheartedly loyal to the country.
slides.imagePrompt: Group of warriors practicing with swords and spears together, bonded and united, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: When the Southern Han army tried to invade again, this 'brotherly' army fought a great battle, liberating Dai La Citadel (Hanoi).
slides.imagePrompt: The army of 3000 warriors charging towards the city gates of Dai La, bravery, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: Duong Dinh Nghe followed the Khuc family's legacy, keeping the flame of autonomy alive for the nation before Ngo Quyen created a great turning point.
slides.imagePrompt: Duong Dinh Nghe sitting with Ngô Quyền (his son-in-law), passing down the spirit of resistance, historical moment, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.question: What special way did Duong Dinh Nghe use to build his forces?
What was Duong Dinh Nghe's army famous for?
